title: How Service Yard Visibility Protects Margin
subheader: Helmful helps service teams see open tickets, work orders, technician assignments, approvals, and hours before small issues become expensive.
featuredImagePrompt: A busy boat dealership service yard with technicians working near boats on trailers, a service manager reviewing a tablet, and organized work bays in the background. Professional documentary style, no text.
body:
The service yard gets expensive when managers cannot see what is stuck.

A service ticket waits on approval. A work order runs over estimated hours. A technician is assigned, but the next step is unclear. The customer calls because nobody has sent an update.

Those are normal service problems. They become margin problems when they are hidden.

What Helmful Does

Helmful gives dealerships a service yard view for open service tickets and work orders by location, technician, status, and hours.

It connects the service ticket, work order, service items, approvals, and customer communication so the team is not managing the yard from a whiteboard and a group text.

How It Helps

Managers can see what needs attention today. Advisors can track approval requests. Technicians have clearer work tied to the job. The dealership can compare estimated hours with actual hours before the invoice becomes a hard conversation.

That helps protect gross margin and reduces customer frustration.

Where To Use It First

Start with open work. Look at service tickets by status, work orders by status, and jobs where actual hours are running ahead of the estimate.

Helmful makes those exceptions easier to find, which is usually where the money is leaking.
